select_statement_syntax.label: SELECT Statement Syntax
select_statement_syntax.summary: Basic structure of a SELECT statement.

sketch2.title: identify the key parts of a SELECT statement

sketch3.title: How to practice SELECT Statement Syntax

concept_overview.title: What is SELECT Statement Syntax?
concept_overview.bodyMarkdown:
SELECT Statement Syntax focuses on this idea: Basic structure of a SELECT statement.

A good starting point is to say the requirement in plain language before writing SQL.

### Learning goals
- write a simple SELECT statement
- identify the key parts of a SELECT statement

goal_one.title: write a simple SELECT statement
goal_one.bodyMarkdown:
write a simple SELECT statement.

When practicing SELECT Statement Syntax, start with a focused example and verify the result carefully.

goal_two.title: identify the key parts of a SELECT statement
goal_two.bodyMarkdown:
identify the key parts of a SELECT statement.

Use the topic idea to explain what the query is doing in plain English.

practice_note.title: How to practice SELECT Statement Syntax
practice_note.bodyMarkdown:
Read the requirement, predict the result, run the query, and verify whether the output really answers the question.

That habit is especially important for SELECT Statement Syntax.

module1_select_statement_syntax_practice.title: Practice: SELECT Statement Syntax
module1_select_statement_syntax_practice.prompt:
The `products` table already exists.

Write a query that shows `name`, `price`, and a calculated column named `price_with_tax` that multiplies `price` by `1.1`.

Then run it.

help.concept: Basic structure of a SELECT statement.
help.hint_1: You can multiply a numeric column directly in the SELECT list.

module1_select_statement_syntax_practice.hint: Use an expression in SELECT and give it an alias.
module1_select_statement_syntax_practice.starterCode:
-- Show a calculated column
SELECT name, price, price * 1.1 AS price_with_tax
FROM products

module1_select_statement_syntax_core_con.title: Core idea: SELECT Statement Syntax
module1_select_statement_syntax_core_con.prompt: What is the best description of SELECT Statement Syntax?

help.concept: Basic structure of a SELECT statement.
help.hint_1: Look for the option that matches the main concept directly.
help.hint_2: Ignore answers that describe unrelated SQL behavior.

module1_select_statement_syntax_core_con.hint: Match the answer to the topic summary.

options.a: It uses expressions in SELECT to create calculated output values.
options.b: It deletes rows before selecting them.

module1_select_statement_syntax_true_sta.title: True statements about SELECT Statement Syntax
module1_select_statement_syntax_true_sta.prompt: Select all true statements about SELECT Statement Syntax.

help.concept: Basic structure of a SELECT statement.
help.hint_1: Pick statements that align with the topic summary and objectives.
help.hint_2: Reject statements that clearly describe unrelated SQL ideas.

module1_select_statement_syntax_true_sta.hint: Use the summary and objectives as your guide.

options.a: You can compute new values directly in SELECT.
options.b: You can give a calculated expression an alias.
options.c: Calculated columns require deleting the original column first.
options.d: Calculated columns only work in UPDATE statements.
options.e: Expressions in SELECT help you inspect transformed output.

module1_select_statement_syntax_workflow.title: Workflow for SELECT Statement Syntax
module1_select_statement_syntax_workflow.prompt: Reorder the workflow ideas for SELECT Statement Syntax.

help.concept: Decide what to calculate, write the expression, then inspect the output.
help.hint_1: Think about what should happen first.
help.hint_2: Then think about what should happen last.

tokens.t1: identify the value you want to calculate
tokens.t2: write the expression in the SELECT list
tokens.t3: run the query and inspect the calculated output
